methods.description: Methods used to examine and analyze the surface features, specifically gnawing marks, on a fossil bone fragment using light microscopy and scanning electron microscopy of a replica.

steps.description: In order to closely observe and examine the marks on the fossil bone, it was studied under a light microscope Leica S8APO (Figure 2b, c and d).
steps.searchable_summary: Examine fossil bone surface features using light microscopy
outputs: Observations of marks on the bone surface

steps.searchable_summary: Create a polyester replica of the fossil bone surface
outputs: Polyester replica of the bone surface

steps.description: and the marks were scanned under the Scanning Electron Microscope-JSM 6490 housed at the Department of Geology, Panjab University, Chandigarh. Multiple grooves measuring between 2 to 5 mm in width and 10 to 12 mm in length are clearly visible (Figure 2f).
steps.searchable_summary: Scan polyester replica using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM)
outputs: Measurements of grooves (2-5 mm width, 10-12 mm length)
